event: emergency admission in critical care unit
blood pressure preserved (mean blood pressure 70-80 mmHg)
chest X-ray showed pneumonia of lower right lobe
echocardiography demonstrated global left ventricle hypokinesia
cutaneous necrotic lesions appeared on extremities, legs, hands, thorax, nose
skin biopsy showed fibrin thrombi and epidermolysis
after 4 days of PE (96 hours from admission)
necrosis extension to roots of members and nose wings
three days after first ECZ injection
ECZ therapy maintained (1200 mg twice monthly)
skin lesions healed on nose and hands
trans-tibial bilateral amputation (3 months after onset)
PCR on stools negative for shigatoxin genes
